reviews.review_text:
Extremely fun AND addicting for the first 500 hours, hundreds of vehicles to build and drive and crash on an array of maps with unrivaled physics.. that being said the mods you can get turn this game into what i think we all want out of every car/racing sim: Real world cars and trucks with engines so customizable you can see and feel the different parts you put on it. maps big enough to drive for hours, and get lost in.

The game is amazing on steering wheel setups, the wheels respond well to the ground you drive on, and simulate realtime oversteer and snap back effects beautifully. the gearboxes act like gearboxes, and correctly behave on the clutch engagment. 

NO it is not perfect, NO it is not replayable for everyone, it is not forza, or assetto corsa, this is more for those who want to change every aspect of their car and see how long it takes to break. wanna gut a semi and blow the engine with 1000kw of nitrous and 100psi of boost? go ahead.. how about mud tires on a ferrari, sure.. and the mods are so, so, beautiful.  

so i 110% recommend this if you want a game like that, but not if you want something like forza, or a true racing sim.
This game changed my life and motivates me to keep pushing forward in life. I genuinely wouldn't know much about cars if it were not for this game. It is fantastic. I play it every. single. day

Super realistic

Put dummy in 20 cars to recreate formula one race 

russel andd antonelli go to take out the ferraris turn one

They do it antonelli and russel have killed everyone

Antonelli and george get bombed from the Redbull army

Woooooooooow!! 

Image a game - future-beam.ng - that is Forza Horizon, Snowrunner and Gran Turismo in ONE :) You'll have giant free roaming landscapes (Forza), a detailed physics model (SR, just wow!) and the tinkering with car parts (GT)!

The pre-relaese version of beam.ng ist very fun to play. You can just try stuff or install the career mod (released also by RLS) and work your way through curier-mission, taxi driving, hauling stuff, drifting, racing, drag-racing....

When this game is ready and RLS will manage a port to console this would be a GIANT banger!!

I play under Linux Mint, steam, vulcan, no problems! Attached a PS5 controller to it :)

Just note that it is pretty CPU demanding for some reason, hopefully Vulkan can fix this.

BeamNG.drive est, selon moi, l’un des meilleurs jeux de simulation automobile jamais créés.

J’y ai passé de nombreuses heures il y a quelques années lorsque je possédais encore mon simulateur, et les souvenirs que j’en garde sont excellents. Les sensations de conduite sont incroyablement réalistes, les réactions mécaniques sont bluffantes, et le moteur physique du jeu reste à ce jour l’un des plus impressionnants que j’ai pu tester.

Ce qui rend BeamNG.drive unique, c’est son niveau de détail tout simplement exceptionnel. On peut personnaliser ses véhicules de fond en comble : modifier les pièces moteur, la transmission, la suspension, l’habitacle, la ligne d’échappement, et bien plus encore. Chaque changement a un impact réel sur le comportement du véhicule, ce qui rend l’expérience aussi technique qu’amusante.

Le jeu peut convenir aussi bien aux passionnés d’automobile qu’aux joueurs qui veulent simplement s’amuser à provoquer des crashs spectaculaires ou à expérimenter toutes sortes de situations improbables. C’est un véritable terrain de jeu mécanique, à la fois sérieux et terriblement divertissant.

Je recommande également vivement d’essayer le mod multijoueur BeamMP. Même s’il n’est pas officiel, il fonctionne remarquablement bien et permet de passer d’excellents moments entre amis. Entre les courses improvisées, les défis absurdes et les accidents en chaîne, les fous rires sont garantis.

BeamNG.drive est un jeu de passion, de précision et de pur plaisir. Un incontournable pour tous les amateurs de simulation automobile.

10/10 ⭐
Un chef-d’œuvre mécanique où chaque crash est une œuvre d’art.

this game is very fun!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! But this game is too realistic, and because it has a lot of physics mechanics and car polygons, it lags!!! But it's fun, right?

BeamNG.drive is one of the best driving simulation games available for car enthusiasts and motorsport fans. The game delivers incredibly realistic handling, industry leading crash physics, and a highly immersive driving experience. Whether you enjoy drifting, track racing, highway cruising, or building custom stance and track cars, BeamNG.drive offers endless possibilities.

One of the strongest aspects of the game is its extensive vehicle customization and modding community. Players can download a huge variety of mods, including realistic cars, maps, tracks, and gameplay enhancements that significantly improve the overall experience. The Nürburgring map mod is especially impressive and provides one of the most enjoyable and realistic track driving experiences available in any driving simulator.

BeamNG.drive is highly recommended for players who appreciate realism, creativity, and freedom in a driving game. However, because of its advanced physics engine and heavy mod support, the game can be demanding on hardware. A decent mid range or high performance PC is recommended, especially if you plan to use multiple mods or realistic graphics settings.

u can do literally everything comes out of mind: crashing cars (obv), speeding around corners in italy, rallying, offroading, cruising, drifting, drag racing, or just running in a straight line finding the max speed of your 1000 HP piccolina u just tuned.
bored? u can customize even more the game with tons of mods available in the repo, or play with ur friends through beammp mod.
though this game lacks some optimization imo, like graphics and cpu usage, u can expect these things bc its running on a relative old game engine and most importantly its still in alpha, but apart from that, not much else to say if not really great game

Love the game. Been here since it first came out in 2013, before Steam and steam greenlight. Development has been a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ recent years and it shows.

Why am I giving it a negative review? I'm not a boot licker. I hold things I love to high standards. Tell me this:

When was the last major gameplay/simulation feature? When was the last vehicle people asked for in the majority? What about the silence lately over updates? Its a 3 month cycle. Been like that for years. Its now almost 6+ months.

Many systems are also outdated past their first iteration.
ABS System
ESC System
Engine Thermal System (0.3 feature btw, never changed substantially since then)
Tire Thermals (nonexistent - teased) 
What about ADAS? Mods had it for YEARS.
Volumetric Clouds?
Proper AA?
Functional Vulkan?
AI that lives up to the standards of the modern century?
Mud? Mods did it first
Dirt on cars? Mods did it first.

Need I go on? Mods are the only reason why this game is alive. Mods are the only reason why the developers have been proven wrong about MP not being possible - which was a LIE over laziness or capability.
A great game for racing and trying to learn how to drive to get a fell of how to be able to drive in the real world.

The best car game. it has everything; fun maps, insanely customisable cars, amazing soft body physics, cool challenges.

And with community mods, you can actually do anything. Want to drive a lawnmower around the Nordshleife, fly a plane into two towers, make the water rise? Well you can do that and much, MUCH more with the mods.

If you like cars, this game is a steal. So stop looking at the reviews and just get it.

Amazing game, I have played for over 800 hours. This game has amazing crash physics, great graphics, which are only getting better every update, great mod support, a great selection of vehicles and customization, a wide variety of highly detailed maps, and so much more great and amazing things. Every vehicle feels unique from one another, no two vehicles feel the same. I never get bored of this game as there is SO much things to do, like I can make mods and maps, make custom configs of vehicles, tune the HELL out of mom vans, and do a bunch of other fun (and stupid) things with the game. While the game may not be perfect for some, in my eyes, its a 10/10.

Repaired the game, deep cleaned the cache, uninstalled old and outdated mods and nothing, try and load up west coast, johnson valley etc. and it'll black screen before loading in. BeamNG.drive is the greatest game ever created for people who look at a perfectly good car and think, "I wonder what would happen if I launched it off that cliff."

On the surface, it's a driving simulator. In reality, it's a highly advanced vehicle destruction simulator disguised as a driving game. The physics are so detailed that every crash feels less like an accident and more like a scientific experiment funded by a government agency with absolutely no oversight.

The driving itself is fantastic. Every vehicle feels different, from tiny hatchbacks to massive trucks. The handling, suspension, weight transfer, and damage modeling are all incredibly well done. You can spend hours just driving around and appreciating how realistic everything feels. Or you can do what most of us do and immediately send a family sedan into a concrete wall at 120 mph to see what happens.

The damage physics are where the game truly shines. Bump a guardrail? Minor damage. Hit a tree? Significant damage. Drive off a mountain while attempting a stunt you had absolutely no chance of landing? Congratulations, your vehicle is now a modern art sculpture.

The amount of content is impressive, and the modding community somehow manages to make an already great game even better. There are countless maps, vehicles, scenarios, and ways to destroy your computer-generated insurance rates.

BeamNG is also one of the few games where failure is often more entertaining than success. You can spend twenty minutes carefully setting up the perfect jump, miss it completely, flip seventeen times, lose every wheel, and still have more fun than if you'd landed it.

The AI can occasionally make decisions that suggest they learned to drive from watching action movies, but honestly, that's part of the charm. Watching traffic create a twenty-car pileup because one vehicle got confused is peak entertainment.

10/10.

I bought a realistic driving simulator and somehow ended up running a crash-testing program that would immediately get shut down by every safety organization on Earth.
